summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: Average kinetic energy of gas particles is proportional to:

KE_avg = (3/2)kT, where T is in Kelvin. Average KE is directly proportional to absolute temperature.

Example: Smell spreading across a room is an example of:

The spreading of scent molecules through air is diffusion — the gradual mixing of one substance through another.

Example: Which gas has the highest average speed at 25°C?

Speed ∝ 1/√M; helium has the lowest molar mass (4 g/mol) so it has the highest average speed.
